title: [PLANNING] NutriLedger — Full Project Roadmap
description:
NutriLedger — Full Project Roadmap

## Active Work
Feature 001: SE API Foundation (qj2) — 21 tasks, all open, start here.
Phase 1 (T001-T003): Install Sanctum, CORS, migrate — READY NOW
Phase 2 (T004-T007): ApiResponses trait, ApiController, exception handler, routes/api.php — blocked by Phase 1
Phase 3 (T008-T010): AuthController, LoginRequest, AuthTest — blocked by Phase 2
Phase 4 (T011-T012): RoleMiddleware verify, RoleAccessTest — blocked by Phase 2
Phase 5 (T013): ResponseContractTest — blocked by Phase 2
Phase 6 (T014-T017): Vite+React scaffold, Axios client, App.jsx ping — blocked by Phase 1
Phase 7 (T018-T021): Quality gates, cleanup, build — blocked by all above

## MVP — SE Track (d0f.1) [P1] — Deadline M2 May 3 / M3 Jun 7 2026
Group 1 — Auth & User Management (est) [READY]
  Login/logout, token auth, admin user CRUD, soft-delete/restore
  BE: AuthController, UserController, UserResource, UserService
  FE: Login page
  [Feature 001 tasks live here — not yet linked]

Group 2 — Patient Management (fg2) [blocked by G1]
  Patient CRUD, medical metadata, socioeconomic profile, search/filter
  BE: PatientController, PatientResource, PatientSocioeconomicResource, PatientService
  FE: Patient list, Create, View, Edit

Group 3 — Visits & Encounters (9d3) [blocked by G2]
  Visit CRUD per patient, doctor notes, patient read-only history
  BE: VisitController, VisitResource, VisitService
  FE: Visit list, Create visit, View visit

M3 — Patterns, Tests & Deployment (jb2) [blocked by G3]
  Service Layer, Repository Pattern, Observer Pattern
  Min 5 Pest HTTP tests, Railway/Fly.io deployment

## Full Platform — SD Track (d0f.2) [P3] — Post-June 2026, blocked by MVP
Group 4 — Vital Signs (0cx): vitals per visit, BMI calc, abnormal flags
Group 5 — Medications (cyj): add/list medications, active/discontinued status
Group 6 — Lab Results (b5j): log labs, chronological view, attach to visits
Group 7 — Recommendations (i6l): visit-based diet/lifestyle recs, expiry tracking
Group 8 — Reminders (lry): schedule follow-up reminders, upcoming view
Group 9 — Dashboards (jh6): clinic dashboard, patient mini-overview widget
Group 10 — Testing & Infrastructure (4ou): validation + factories + seeders for G4-9
Group 11 — Finalization (13m): route/role audit, schema index review, staging deploy

## AI & Automation Layer (d0f.3) [P4] — blocked by MVP + Group 6
Low:    Nutritional risk scoring (rule-based), BMI trend chart
Low-M:  Inactivity/follow-up alerts (n8n)
Medium: Menu suggestions v1 (rule-based), pre-visit briefing (n8n+LLM),
        Doctor assistant Phase 1 — single patient RAG (Laravel AI SDK + Claude, SSE streaming),
        Scheduled report digest (n8n email)
Med-H:  Doctor assistant Phase 2 — cross-patient queries, menu suggestions v2 (LLM),
        PDF report generation (queued job + DomPDF)
Medium: Voice-to-notes (Whisper API + React mic)
High:   Lab result interpretation — requires Group 6

## Gaps
- Feature 001 (qj2) not yet linked to Group 1 epic (est) — wire after 001 completes
- Groups 4-11 have no inter-group order deps — wire when speccing each group
- Groups 2, 3 and all SD groups have no tasks yet — run /speckit.specify + /speckit.tasks per group

title: Fix camelCase request keys in StorePatientRequest and PatientApiTest
description:
Constitution Principle VI (v2.1.0) mandates snake_case for all request body inputs. StorePatientRequest was written with camelCase rule keys (userId, firstName, dateOfBirth, etc.) before this decision was made. PatientApiTest sends matching camelCase payloads. Both must be corrected so that $request->validated() can be passed directly to PatientService without any conversion layer.

What to change:
1. app/Http/Requests/StorePatientRequest.php — rename all 19 rule keys from camelCase to snake_case (userId→user_id, firstName→first_name, dateOfBirth→date_of_birth, etc. including all socioeconomic.* nested keys). Full mapping in specs/003-patient-management-api/camelcase-remediation.md.
2. tests/Feature/Patient/PatientApiTest.php — update all postJson/patchJson payloads to snake_case. Update assertJsonValidationErrors calls (first_name not firstName, date_of_birth not dateOfBirth). Do NOT touch response assertions (assertJsonPath/assertJsonStructure on data.attributes.* keys) — those are camelCase and correct.

What NOT to change:
- PatientResource.php and PatientSocioeconomicResource.php — camelCase output is correct.
- PatientService.php — convention-neutral, no changes.

Verification: php artisan test tests/Feature/Patient/PatientApiTest.php && vendor/bin/pint --dirty && composer run analyse
close_reason: StorePatientRequest rule keys converted to snake_case (19 keys). PatientApiTest payloads and assertJsonValidationErrors updated to match. Response assertions (camelCase) unchanged. Pint clean.

title: T015: [US1] Test admin can view single patient with socioeconomic
description:
## Test Case
Verify admin can view a single patient and response includes socioeconomic data in the included array.

## File
tests/Feature/Patient/PatientApiTest.php

## Test Design
```php
it('allows admin to view single patient with socioeconomic', function () {
    $admin = User::factory()->admin()->create();
    $patient = Patient::factory()->hasSocioeconomic()->create();

    $response = $this->actingAs($admin)
        ->getJson("/api/patients/{$patient->id}");

    $response->assertOk()
        ->assertJsonStructure([
            'data' => [
                'type',
                'id',
                'attributes' => [
                    'firstName', 'lastName', 'fullName', 'dateOfBirth',
                    'gender', 'phone', 'bloodType', 'allergies', 'medicalNotes',
                ],
                'relationships' => [
                    'socioeconomic' => ['data'],
                    'user' => ['data'],
                ],
            ],
            'included',
        ])
        ->assertJsonPath('data.type', 'patient')
        ->assertJsonPath('data.id', (string) $patient->id);
});
```

## Acceptance
- [ ] Creates patient with socioeconomic via factory
- [ ] Asserts 200 OK
- [ ] Asserts JSON:API data with type='patient'
- [ ] Asserts all expected attribute keys present (camelCase)
- [ ] Asserts relationships block contains socioeconomic and user
- [ ] Asserts included array is present
close_reason: Closed

title: [Phase 3] US1: Admin/Doctor Patient CRUD (MVP)
description:
## User Story
As an Admin or Doctor I can create, list, view, update, and soft-delete patient records so I can manage patient care and records.

## Priority: P1 (MVP)

## Independent Test
Using authenticated Admin/Doctor credentials, perform full CRUD via API and validate responses and persisted data.

## Acceptance Scenarios
1. Admin POST valid patient + socioeconomic data → 201 with patient resource including socioeconomic
2. Doctor GET /patients → paginated list with relationships loaded
3. Admin DELETE patient → soft-deleted, excluded from subsequent GET

## Test Coverage Required (11 tests)
- Admin create patient with socioeconomic (201)
- Doctor create patient with socioeconomic (201)
- Admin list all patients with pagination
- Doctor list all patients with relationships
- Admin view single patient with socioeconomic
- Admin update patient fields
- Admin soft-delete patient
- Soft-deleted patient excluded from list
- Unauthenticated request returns 401
- Validation errors return 422 with field messages
- Response conforms to JSON:API envelope

## Implementation Tasks
- Implement PatientService::createPatient()
- Implement PatientService::updatePatient()
- Implement PatientController::index()
- Implement PatientController::store()
- Implement PatientController::show()
- Implement PatientController::update()
- Implement PatientController::destroy()

## Checkpoint
After this phase, Admin/Doctor CRUD is fully functional and independently testable.
close_reason: Closed

title: T010: Verify PatientPolicy authorization rules
notes:
DESIGN CONFLICT — requires decision before implementation.

Current code: PatientPolicy::viewAny() returns false for pacijent role.
Existing passing test (PatientPolicyTest.php:22): asserts viewAny IS false for pacijent.

T010 spec + T008 controller design: viewAny must return TRUE for all roles, controller index() filters by user_id for patient role.

Two valid approaches:
A) Filter in policy (current): viewAny=false for patient. Controller never reaches the query. PatientPolicyTest stays green. But controller cannot use authorize('viewAny') for patients — needs different flow.
B) Filter in controller (spec): viewAny=true for all roles. Controller filters. PatientPolicyTest line 22 must be updated to assert TRUE. Cleaner separation of concerns.

The T008 controller design assumes approach B. Choose before starting T010 or T008.
close_reason: Policy fully implemented in c7775e4 (soft deletes + auth matrix). viewAny=true for all roles, controller filters. restore() added for admin/doctor. All acceptance criteria met.

title: T007: Update UpdatePatientRequest validation rules
description: Update app/Http/Requests/UpdatePatientRequest.php with sometimes rules and user_id protection. All rule keys must be snake_case (Constitution Principle VI). See DESIGN field for the complete rules array.
design:
```php
public function authorize(): bool
{
    return $this->user()->can('update', $this->route('patient'));
}

public function rules(): array
{
    return [
        // Patient fields - sometimes (only validate if present)
        'first_name' => ['sometimes', 'string', 'max:50'],
        'last_name' => ['sometimes', 'string', 'max:50'],
        'date_of_birth' => ['sometimes', 'date', 'before:today'],
        'gender' => ['sometimes', 'in:M,F'],
        'phone' => ['sometimes', 'string', 'max:33'],
        'address' => ['sometimes', 'nullable', 'string', 'max:100'],
        'city' => ['sometimes', 'nullable', 'string', 'max:33'],
        'postal_code' => ['sometimes', 'nullable', 'string', 'max:20'],
        'emergency_contact_name' => ['sometimes', 'string', 'max:100'],
        'emergency_contact_phone' => ['sometimes', 'string', 'max:50'],
        'blood_type' => ['sometimes', 'nullable', 'in:A+,A-,B+,B-,AB+,AB-,O+,O-'],
        'allergies' => ['sometimes', 'nullable', 'string'],
        'medical_notes' => ['sometimes', 'nullable', 'string'],

        // user_id is FORBIDDEN on update
        'user_id' => ['prohibited'],

        // Socioeconomic fields
        'socioeconomic' => ['sometimes', 'array'],
        'socioeconomic.marital_status' => ['sometimes', 'nullable', 'in:single,married,divorced,widowed,separated,other'],
        'socioeconomic.number_of_dependents' => ['sometimes', 'nullable', 'integer', 'min:0'],
        'socioeconomic.employment_status' => ['sometimes', 'nullable', 'in:employed_full_time,employed_part_time,self_employed,unemployed,retired,student,unable_to_work,other'],
        'socioeconomic.income_level' => ['sometimes', 'nullable', 'in:low,lower_middle,middle,upper_middle,high'],
        'socioeconomic.has_health_insurance' => ['sometimes', 'nullable', 'boolean'],
        'socioeconomic.smoking_status' => ['sometimes', 'nullable', 'in:never,former,current_light,current_heavy'],
        'socioeconomic.alcohol_consumption' => ['sometimes', 'nullable', 'in:none,occasional,moderate,heavy'],
        'socioeconomic.physical_activity_level' => ['sometimes', 'nullable', 'in:sedentary,lightly_active,moderately_active,very_active'],
        'socioeconomic.food_security_status' => ['sometimes', 'nullable', 'in:food_secure,food_insecure,unsure'],
        'socioeconomic.additional_notes' => ['sometimes', 'nullable', 'string'],
    ];
}
```
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] authorize() checks Patient update policy with route model binding
- [ ] All patient fields use sometimes rule with snake_case keys
- [ ] user_id field is prohibited (returns 422 if sent)
- [ ] All socioeconomic nested fields use snake_case keys

title: T006: Update StorePatientRequest validation rules

## Task
Update app/Http/Requests/StorePatientRequest.php with comprehensive validation

```php
public function authorize(): bool
{
    return $this->user()->can('create', Patient::class);
}

public function rules(): array
{

        // Patient core fields
        'firstName' => ['required', 'string', 'max:255'],
        'lastName' => ['required', 'string', 'max:255'],

        'city' => ['nullable', 'string', 'max:255'],
        'postalCode' => ['nullable', 'string', 'max:20'],
        'emergencyContactName' => ['required', 'string', 'max:255'],
        'emergencyContactPhone' => ['required', 'string', 'max:50'],
        'bloodType' => ['nullable', 'in:A+,A-,B+,B-,AB+,AB-,O+,O-'],
        'allergies' => ['nullable', 'string'],
        'medicalNotes' => ['nullable', 'string'],

        // Socioeconomic fields (all optional on create)
        'socioeconomic' => ['nullable', 'array'],
        'socioeconomic.maritalStatus' => ['nullable', 'in:single,married,divorced,widowed,separated,other'],
        'socioeconomic.numberOfDependents' => ['nullable', 'integer', 'min:0'],
        'socioeconomic.employmentStatus' => ['nullable', 'in:employed_full_time,employed_part_time,self_employed,unemployed,retired,student,unable_to_work,other'],
        'socioeconomic.incomeLevel' => ['nullable', 'in:low,lower_middle,middle,upper_middle,high'],
        'socioeconomic.hasHealthInsurance' => ['nullable', 'boolean'],
        'socioeconomic.smokingStatus' => ['nullable', 'in:never,former,current_light,current_heavy'],
        'socioeconomic.alcoholConsumption' => ['nullable', 'in:none,occasional,moderate,heavy'],
        'socioeconomic.physicalActivityLevel' => ['nullable', 'in:sedentary,lightly_active,moderately_active,very_active'],
        'socioeconomic.foodSecurityStatus' => ['nullable', 'in:food_secure,food_insecure,unsure'],
        'socioeconomic.additionalNotes' => ['nullable', 'string'],
    ];
}
```

## Note on userId mapping
The controller must map the camelCase 'userId' key to snake_case 'user_id' before passing to Patient::create(). Use array_key mapping or a dedicated prepareForValidation() method.

## Acceptance
- [ ] authorize() checks Patient create policy
- [ ] userId is required and must exist in users table
- [ ] All patient core fields validated with appropriate rules
- [ ] All socioeconomic enum fields have in: validation
- [ ] Nested socioeconomic array supported

title: T005: Create PatientSocioeconomicResource (JSON:API compliant)
description:
## Task
Create app/Http/Resources/Api/PatientSocioeconomicResource.php with JSON:API v1 compliance

## Design
```php
namespace App\Http\Resources\Api;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Http\Resources\Json\JsonResource;

                'numberOfDependents' => $this->number_of_dependents,
                'livingArrangement' => $this->living_arrangement,
                'employmentStatus' => $this->employment_status,
                'occupation' => $this->occupation,
                'incomeLevel' => $this->income_level,
                'hasHealthInsurance' => $this->has_health_insurance,
                'educationLevel' => $this->education_level,
                'smokingStatus' => $this->smoking_status,
                'alcoholConsumption' => $this->alcohol_consumption,
                'physicalActivityLevel' => $this->physical_activity_level,
                'hasFamilySupport' => $this->has_family_support,
                'hasCaregiver' => $this->has_caregiver,
                'transportationAccess' => $this->transportation_access,
                'foodSecurityStatus' => $this->food_security_status,
                'dietaryRestrictionsCultural' => $this->dietary_restrictions_cultural,
                'additionalNotes' => $this->additional_notes,
                'createdAt' => $this->created_at?->toIso8601String(),
                'updatedAt' => $this->updated_at?->toIso8601String(),
            ],
        ];
    }
}
```

description:
Define the full route structure for the API: a public ping endpoint, authenticated auth routes, and empty role group skeletons. The skeletons are intentionally empty — real routes are added in features 002-patients and 003-visits.

(1) GET /api/ping — public, no middleware, closure returning ok envelope with {status: "ok"}. This is the CORS smoke test endpoint and MUST have no middleware.
(2) POST /api/login — no auth middleware (the user is not authenticated yet at this point).
(3) Under auth:sanctum middleware: POST /api/logout (AuthController@logout), GET /api/user (AuthController@me).
(4) Under auth:sanctum + role:admin: empty group with comment "User management — 002-users BE".
(5) Under auth:sanctum + role:admin,doktor: empty group with comment "Patients + Visits — 002-patients, 003-visits BE".

KEY FACTS:
- routes/api.php is auto-loaded by Laravel 12 when registered via withRouting(api:) in bootstrap/app.php.
- RoleMiddleware is already registered as the "role" alias — call it as middleware("role:admin").
- AuthController does not need to exist yet — routes can reference it before the class is created.

DEPENDS ON: T005 (ApiController must exist) and T006 (exception handling must be in place before auth routes are tested).

VERIFY: php artisan route:list --path=api — ping, login, logout, and user must all be listed.

title: T006: Global exception handling in bootstrap/app.php
description:
Ensure Laravel returns JSON — not HTML redirects — for two critical exception types on API routes.

PROBLEM WITHOUT THIS: when auth:sanctum rejects a request, Fortify fires a redirect to /login (a web route). The React SPA receives a 302 HTML response it cannot parse — silent failure that is confusing to debug.

SOLUTION: add exception handlers inside the withExceptions() closure in bootstrap/app.php. Laravel 12 has no app/Exceptions/Handler.php — bootstrap/app.php is the correct location.

HANDLER 1 — AuthenticationException:
  Condition: $request->is("api/*") or $request->expectsJson()
  Return: response()->json(["message" => "Unauthenticated.", "status" => 401, "data" => null], 401)

HANDLER 2 — ValidationException:
  Return: response()->json(["message" => $e->getMessage(), "errors" => $e->errors()], 422)

IMPORTANT: the intercept must be selective — only API routes return JSON. Web routes must keep their existing redirect behaviour.

TDD: write the 401 test before implementing (getJson /api/user without a token must return 401 JSON with {message, status, data} keys — NOT an HTML redirect). Add this test to AuthTest.php (created in T008).

DEPENDS ON: T004 — the response format must be defined before the exception handler can mirror it.
close_reason: AuthenticationException 401, ValidationException 422 with errors, generic HttpException handler all wired in bootstrap/app.php

Create a minimal base controller that all API controllers extend. Its only purpose is to carry the ApiResponses trait so subclasses inherit all response helpers without each declaring "use ApiResponses" themselves.

INHERITANCE CHAIN: ApiController extends Controller -> AuthController extends ApiController.

COMMAND: php artisan make:controller Api/ApiController --no-interaction

MODIFY the generated class to:
- Extend App\Http\Controllers\Controller
- Add: use App\Traits\ApiResponses;
- No constructor, no methods — the class body is just the trait declaration. Keep it empty.

DEPENDS ON: T004 must be complete before this task — ApiController cannot reference the trait until it exists.

VERIFY: php artisan route:list --path=api (no errors = class is loadable), vendor/bin/pint --dirty.

title: T002: Configure CORS in config/cors.php
description: Add http://localhost:5173 to allowed_origins so the React SPA (Vite dev server, port 5173) can make requests to the Laravel API (port 8000) without browser Same-Origin Policy errors. KEY CONCEPT: browsers enforce SOP — a page on port 5173 cannot fetch from port 8000 unless the server sends correct CORS headers. Laravel's HandleCors middleware reads config/cors.php at runtime and handles preflight OPTIONS automatically — no extra packages needed. Check: allowed_methods includes * or explicit list, allowed_headers includes Authorization (needed for Bearer token). supports_credentials must be false for Bearer token auth (not cookie-based). VERIFY: curl -i -X OPTIONS http://localhost:8000/api/ping -H 'Origin: http://localhost:5173' -H 'Access-Control-Request-Method: GET' — response must include Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://localhost:5173. ## User Story
As a Patient I can view and update my own profile and socioeconomic details so I can keep my information current.

## Priority: P2

## Independent Test
Authenticate as patient and GET/PATCH own resource; verify 403 for other patient IDs.

## Acceptance Scenarios
1. Patient GET /patients/{id} for own ID → 200 with resource
2. Patient GET /patients/{id} for other ID → 403 Forbidden
3. Patient PATCH own record → 200 with updated resource
4. Patient GET /patients → returns only own record

## Test Coverage Required (6 tests)
- Patient can view own record
- Patient cannot view other patient records (403)
- Patient can update own profile
- Patient cannot update other patient records (403)
- Patient list returns only own record
- Patient cannot delete any patient (403)

## Implementation
- Update PatientController::index() to filter by user when patient role
- Verify PatientPolicy view/update rules for patient self-access

title: T017: Configure .env.local and .env.example in /frontend
description: Set up environment variable configuration so any developer can clone the repo and point the SPA to the correct API URL. FILES: frontend/.env.example (committed to git, template) and frontend/.env.local (gitignored, local dev values). CONTENT OF BOTH: NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:8000. NOTE: Next.js uses NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix (not VITE_) to expose vars to client-side code. Next.js reads from .env.local (not .env) for local dev — .env.local is gitignored by default by create-next-app. VERIFY .env.local IS GITIGNORED: check frontend/.gitignore — add manually if missing. NEVER commit .env.local. .env.example IS committed — it is the onboarding template new developers copy. HOW NEXT.JS READS IT: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL is available in client components. Non-NEXT_PUBLIC_ vars are server-side only. PARALLEL with T015 and T016. DEPENDS ON T014.

title: T015: Create Axios client instance in frontend/src/api/client.ts
description: Build a pre-configured Axios instance that ALL API calls in the SPA will use. FILE: frontend/src/api/client.ts (TypeScript, not .js). IMPLEMENT: (1) const client = axios.create({ baseURL: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL, headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'Accept': 'application/json' } }); (2) request interceptor: client.interceptors.request.use(config => { const token = localStorage.getItem('token'); if (token) { config.headers.Authorization = 'Bearer ' + token; } return config; }); (3) export default client. HOW NEXT.JS ENV WORKS: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL reads from .env.local at build/runtime — only NEXT_PUBLIC_-prefixed vars are exposed to client code (replaces Vite's import.meta.env.VITE_ convention). INTERCEPTOR IS A STUB: no token stored yet — wired now so auth feature just stores token and it works automatically. PARALLEL with T016 and T017. DEPENDS ON T014 (scaffold must exist first).

title: [Phase 5] US3: Incremental Socioeconomic Data Capture
description:
## User Story
As a clinician or patient I can add or update socioeconomic fields independently from patient core fields so data can be collected progressively.

## Priority: P3

## Independent Test
Update only socioeconomic subset and verify other patient fields unchanged; socioeconomic record created if absent.

## Acceptance Scenario
Given existing patient with no socioeconomic record, when clinician PATCHes socioeconomic data, then a socioeconomic record is created and linked.

## Test Coverage Required (5 tests)
- PATCH with only socioeconomic fields creates record if absent
- PATCH with socioeconomic fields updates existing record
- Partial socioeconomic payload only updates provided fields
- Patient core fields unchanged when only socioeconomic updated
- Socioeconomic enum validation (invalid values return 422)

## Implementation
- Enhance PatientService::updatePatient() to create socioeconomic if absent
- Ensure UpdatePatientRequest validates socioeconomic enums
close_reason: Closed

title: T013: Write response contract tests (all envelope shapes)
description:
One integration test per response type, asserting the exact JSON envelope shape. These are end-to-end — they hit real routes with no mocking. The goal is to prove that the entire system consistently produces the correct format for every possible response scenario.

FILE: tests/Feature/Api/ResponseContractTest.php
COMMAND: php artisan make:test Api/ResponseContractTest --pest --no-interaction

TESTS TO WRITE:
(1) 200 success — GET /api/ping, assertOk() + assertJsonStructure(["message", "status", "data"])
(2) 422 validation — POST /api/login with empty body, assertUnprocessable() + assertJsonStructure(["message", "errors"]) + assertJsonPath("errors.email.0", fn($v) => is_string($v))
(3) 401 unauthenticated — GET /api/user without token, assertUnauthorized() + assertJsonStructure(["message", "status", "data"])
(4) 403 wrong role — authenticated pacijent hits GET /api/test/admin-only, assertForbidden() + assertJsonStructure(["message", "status", "data"])
(5) 404 unknown route — GET /api/nonexistent, assert 404 + assertJsonStructure(["message", "status", "data"])

NOTE ON TEST (4): the /api/test/admin-only stub route is added by T012 (Phase 4). This test cannot run until T012 is complete.

NOTE ON TEST (5): check whether Laravel returns JSON or HTML for unknown api/* routes by default. If HTML, add a NotFoundHttpException handler in bootstrap/app.php alongside the existing AuthenticationException handler from T006.

ASSERTION GUIDE: assertJsonStructure() verifies keys exist; assertJsonPath() verifies a specific value at a path.
